key_quote: The alphabet abstraction over natural language relies on subsymbolic embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2), creating tension between the symbolic formal guarantees and the approximate, clustering-based foundation they rest on
rationale: All five reviewers acknowledge the novelty of applying coalgebraic theory to LLM behavioral auditing — if the implementation were made rigorous with Phase 0 results, Lean formalization, and demonstrated scalability, reviewers would not say 'not novel.' The primary blockers are execution gaps: undemonstrated practical scalability, missing Phase 0 empirical validation, infeasible Lean formalization, and classifier dependency errors. This is Category B: novel idea, incomplete execution.
